name: Hisense U8N 65-Inch 4K Mini-LED ULED Smart TV (2024)

specs.HDR: Dolby Vision IQ, HDR10+, HLG — up to 3000 nits peak

whatWorks: The U8N's 1000+ dimming zone Mini-LED panel hits up to 3000 nits peak brightness — a spec that puts it in genuine competition with TVs costing $2,000+. HDR content, particularly Dolby Vision mastered films on Apple TV+ and Disney+, looks absolutely spectacular with real specular highlights and inky blacks.

144Hz native refresh rate combined with ALLM and VRR via all four HDMI 2.1 ports makes this one of the best gaming TVs under $1,000 in Canada. PS5 and Xbox Series X both run at 4K 120Hz simultaneously without swapping cables.

At $999 CAD, the U8N is routinely compared favorably to TVs at $1,500–$2,000 in independent reviews — Rtings rates it competitively with the Samsung QN90 and LG QNED99 at significantly higher price points. The value proposition is exceptional.

Google TV's content discovery and performance on the U8N is strong — smooth navigation, universal search across Crave, Netflix, Disney+, and Prime, and Chromecast built in for phone mirroring. The AI-upscaling applied to 1080p and SDR content also noticeably sharpens older material.
worthKnowing: Peak brightness at 3000 nits is a measured highlight — sustained full-screen brightness is lower and the TV will engage thermal throttling if maintained at maximum brightness for extended periods. In practice this doesn't affect typical viewing but is worth knowing for measurement enthusiasts.

Hisense's long-term software support has historically been weaker than LG and Samsung — Google TV mitigates this somewhat since Google handles OS updates, but Hisense-specific features and firmware may not see the same update cadence.

Some units exhibit minor uniformity issues on bright static backgrounds — not visible on normal content but perceptible on spreadsheets or white UI screens. Sample variation is real; check the return window if uniformity matters for your use case.
The included remote is basic and not backlit. Hisense sells an upgraded remote separately, or buyers can use the Google TV app on their phone as a temporary alternative.

commonComplaints.title: Uniformity issue on mine…
commonComplaints.body: My unit has a slightly brighter patch in the upper right corner on white backgrounds. Not visible on normal TV content but annoying on web browsing.

commonComplaints.title: Thermal throttling at max brightness…
commonComplaints.body: The TV dims itself after extended bright scenes. Hisense says this is normal but feels like the advertised 3000 nits is a peak-peak number not sustained.

commonComplaints.title: Remote feels cheap…
commonComplaints.body: For a $1000 TV the remote is plasticky and hard to read in the dark. LG's Magic Remote at this price point is a completely different experience.

commonComplaints.title: Worried about long-term software support…
commonComplaints.body: Hisense dropped updates for my last TV after 2 years. Hoping the Google TV base means better support here, but I'm watching it.

verdict: The Hisense U8N is the most impressive value in the Canadian TV market — 1000+ zone Mini-LED, 3000 nit peak brightness, 144Hz, four HDMI 2.1 ports, and Dolby Vision IQ for $999 CAD. It competes picture-quality-wise with TVs costing $500 more and is the closest thing to OLED contrast at a non-OLED price. Minor concerns about long-term software support and sample variation in uniformity are the only notable caveats.

realBuyers.quote: I compared this side-by-side with the LG C4 OLED at my friend's place. The OLED wins in absolute dark scenes but the U8N at $600 less is almost as good.
Every HDR movie I watch on this TV looks like a theatrical experience. The highlights literally sparkle on explosions and sunlit scenes.
144Hz gaming is buttery smooth. PS5 runs at 4K 120Hz and the input lag is imperceptible.
I've owned Samsung, LG, and Sony TVs. This Hisense at $999 beats all of them on raw picture performance. Hisense has arrived.

metaDescription: Hisense U8N 65-inch review Canada: 1000+ zone Mini-LED, 3000 nits, 144Hz, 4x HDMI 2.1 at $999 CAD. Near-OLED performance at LCD price.
introText: The Hisense U8N 65-inch (ASIN B0CNHHB77L) is a Mini-LED powerhouse with 1000+ dimming zones, 3000-nit peak brightness, and 144Hz native — available on Amazon.ca for around $999 CAD.
